search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk ng76 child abuse neglect record actual words non-leading assessment
site:who.int WHO RHR 14.26 safeguarding documentation partner violence
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic abuse, neglect or vulnerability conclusion
automatic police, social-care, shelter or safeguarding referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Safeguarding concern requires careful clinician assessment and exact population context.
Reporting and action depend on immediate safety, consent where applicable, UAE law and local pathways.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Exact evidence covers children/young people and adult women experiencing IPV; vulnerable adults, men, elders, trafficking and other safeguarding contexts remain incompletely covered.
setting_applicability: Emergency recognition, assessment and documentation of safeguarding concern.
UAE_applicability: Communication principles transfer, but UAE mandatory reporting, police, social support, child protection, consent and confidentiality law and pathways govern.
recency_verification: NICE NG76 (2017) and WHO handbook (2014) exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14; material recency and population limits remain.
superseded_check: Current registered sources identified; no single authoritative all-population safeguarding source was found.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: For children and young people, the exact NICE section supports recording patient concerns, consent-based assessment and completed risk or consent discussion in the child’s actual words where appropriate.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports child-centred, private and accessible communication, consent to examination, and recording actual words and disagreements.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: For children and young people, the exact recognition section supports associated context, relevant negatives, clinician risk context, safeguarding concern and clinician-completed action without asserting abuse.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recognising indirect disclosure, using open non-leading questions, explaining next steps and recording local safeguarding referral when clinician-completed.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact child-assessment section supports focused assessment, appearance, clinician plan, specialist discussion and arranged handover only when performed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports observing the child and interactions, communicating directly and privately where appropriate, non-leading exploration and structured family or care-environment context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: For adult women experiencing intimate-partner violence, the exact WHO documentation section supports consent-based recording of concerns, symptoms, signs and injuries; it does not cover all adult safeguarding groups.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports asking permission to document and recording complaints, symptoms, signs, injuries and relevant history in the person’s words without interpretation.

unresolved_source_gaps: Vulnerable adults, men, elder abuse, trafficking, exact disclosure, immediate danger, dependants, capacity, injuries, forensic needs, reporting law, referral and safety planning remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
